title: fumaria capreolata subsp. dubia
intro: Fumaria capreolata subsp. dubia, also known as Dubious Fumitory, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the fumitory family, characterized by its distinctive, delicate appearance and yellow flowers. Traditionally, Dubious Fumitory has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its potential anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, including relieving respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as aiding in the treatment of gastrointestinal problems and skin conditions. Dubious Fumitory can be prepared into various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, although its efficacy and safety as a medicinal agent require further research and validation.
